$comment: THEIA - the Mars-sized impactor of the giant-impact hypothesis (docs/27: the birth of the Moon). Declared as a differentiated Mars-like body: iron core, peridotite mantle, hot from accretion. Layer masses integrate to ~6.5e23 kg (Mars-scale, the theorized impactor class). SURFACE TEMPERATURE, corrected: this declared 1,200 K, which made Theia arrive GLOWING - and it cannot. 1,200 K is below peridotite's 1,393 K melt point, so the surface is solid rock, yet it would radiate 118 kW/m2, about 87x what a sunlit surface reflects. Nothing sustains that on a Mars-sized body 60 Myr after formation: a surface that hot sheds its heat in geological no-time, and what is left is a chilled crust over a hot interior - the Moon and Mars both look like that today. So the surface is 300 K, near the ~269 K radiative equilibrium of a dark body at 1 AU, with the mantle still at 1,800 K underneath. Theia therefore approaches as DARK ROCK LIT BY THE SUN, and glows only where the impact makes it glow - which is the whole point of deriving emission from temperature.
name: Theia
